findings: Specific numbers in the title are mandatory — '$30K', '3 years', '500 pages'. Vague = ignored
Personal story framing beats tips/advice posts every time
Honest failure or counterintuitive outcome outperforms success stories
Best post times: Tue–Thu, 9–11 AM EST (2–4 PM UTC)
New accounts: comment on hot posts within first 30 min to get visibility while post is trending
Add genuine data points in comments ('I ran this and got X result') — gets upvoted fast
Do NOT link your product in comments for first 3 months / 100+ karma
Shadow-banning is real — avoid any promotional language in first 5 posts
Best tags: #showdev #ai #webdev #productivity (max 4 tags)
Always add a cover image — postless articles are invisible in feeds
Short read time wins: 2–5 min articles get more reactions than 9+ min
Add #discuss tag for extra visibility in discussion feed
First 2 lines below title are shown in feed — make line 1 a hook
Title must make a specific claim or create a curiosity gap
Top reaction articles use emotional resonance + 'nobody is talking about this' framing
Never put external link in first tweet — X suppresses reach. Put link in reply or last tweet
Always include a screenshot/image — 3–4x more impressions
Best times: Tue–Thu 8–10 AM EST
Engage genuinely with @levelsio, @marc_louvion, @yongfook BEFORE asking for anything
Use #buildinpublic and reply to active threads with useful insights
Threads outperform single tweets for virality — hook → 3–7 points → link at end
Hook must have specific number or counterintuitive claim to get RTs
Find a hunter with 1000+ followers 2–4 weeks before launch — DM them directly
Launch at exactly 12:01 AM PST for max 24-hour window
First 2 hours are critical — get 50+ upvotes fast or algorithm buries you
Maker comment must be personal and detailed — not a product description
Have 20+ maker friends ready to upvote and comment on launch day
Post teaser content on X with screenshots 2 weeks before launch
Reply to every single comment on launch day — engagement boosts ranking

titleFormulas: "[Specific Loss/Cost]. [What Changed Because of It]." → 'My landing page was costing me 40% of visitors. Here's what the data showed.'
"Analyzed [N] landing pages with AI. Here's what actually kills conversion." → Specific number + counterintuitive finding
"Your beautiful landing page might be killing your conversions. Here's proof." → Controversial claim that challenges belief
"Built an AI that reads your landing page and tells you why people leave" (DEV.to style)
"I Think a Lot of Founders Are Quietly Embarrassed By Their Landing Pages" (emotional resonance)
"I analyzed 847 landing pages. Here's what separates the ones that convert:" (specific number hook for Twitter)
"Built PageScore in a weekend. Analyzed 100 pages. Here's the one thing they all got wrong." (build-in-public + finding)
postingTimes: Reddit: Tuesday–Thursday, 9–11 AM EST (14:00–16:00 UTC) — US morning peak
Reddit: Avoid Friday afternoon and Saturday entirely
Twitter: Tuesday–Thursday, 8–10 AM EST for max reach
DEV.to: Less time-sensitive — quality + tags matter more than timing
Product Hunt: Launch at exactly 12:01 AM PST on a Tuesday
keyInsights: Specific numbers beat vague claims every time — '847 pages' > 'hundreds of pages'
Failure stories and honest accounting consistently outperform success stories on Reddit
On Twitter, put the link in a reply — not the first tweet — to avoid reach suppression
DEV.to's #discuss tag gets featured in a dedicated feed — always include it
For new Reddit accounts: give genuine value in comments on trending posts within first 30 min
Product Hunt: first 2 hours are make or break — have a launch team ready
Build-in-public tweets with before/after numbers get saved and RT'd more than any other format
73% of landing pages analyzed have the same problem: no clear CTA above the fold — use this as our hook
doNot: Don't link PageScore in Reddit comments until account has 100+ karma
Don't post 'Check out my tool!' — always wrap it in a story or finding
Don't use bullet-list 'tips' posts on Reddit — smells like content farm, gets ignored
Don't put external links in the first tweet — X will suppress reach
Don't post on Reddit Friday afternoons or Saturdays
Don't use vague titles — 'Some thoughts on landing pages' gets zero upvotes
Don't post on Product Hunt without a pre-built launch team of 20+ supporters
Don't write DEV.to articles over 9 minutes — shorter gets more reactions
